---
title: "collaboration_guide"
output: html_document
---

## Description

What a pull request is and why review matters?

## Methods
To understand why pull requests matter on GitHub, we followed a standard collaborative workflow. First, a new branch was created from the main branch to isolate changes related to a specific issue. Edits and additions were made locally, then staged, committed with descriptive messages, and pushed to the remote repository. A pull request (PR) was then opened to propose merging these changes back into the main branch.

## Discussion
Pull requests are essential because they create a structured checkpoint between writing code and integrating it into a shared project. They promote collaboration by allowing team members to review each other’s work, catch errors early, and suggest improvements before changes become permanent.
